title: Base rate card
front: Out of 1000 people, 10 have Condition X.
back: This is the prior probability before the test result.
teacher_use: Use in sequence to build the Bayesian update from concrete frequencies to notation.

title: True positives card
front: The test correctly flags 9 of the 10 people with Condition X.
back: These 9 belong in the numerator for P(H | +).
teacher_use: Use in sequence to build the Bayesian update from concrete frequencies to notation.

title: False positives card
front: The test falsely flags 99 people who do not have Condition X.
back: These cases join the positive-test denominator.
teacher_use: Use in sequence to build the Bayesian update from concrete frequencies to notation.

title: Update card
front: After a positive test, use P(H | +) = 9 / (9 + 99) = 1 / 12 ≈ 8.3%.
back: Point to the denominator: all positive tests, not all real cases.
teacher_use: Use in sequence to build the Bayesian update from concrete frequencies to notation.

title: Notation trap card
front: P(H | +) asks about the hypothesis after the positive result.
back: Do not confuse it with P(+ | H), the chance of a positive result if the hypothesis is true.
teacher_use: Use in sequence to build the Bayesian update from concrete frequencies to notation.

title: Communication card
front: Which format is clearest: percentage, frequency, grid, or formula?
back: Students evaluate responsible communication, not just calculation.
teacher_use: Use in sequence to build the Bayesian update from concrete frequencies to notation.

title: Question 1
front: Write one claim in a complete sentence.
back: Teacher cue: look for a clear claim, not just a description.
teacher_use: Students answer before the teacher reveal.

title: Question 2
back: Teacher cue: students should name visible words, data, source features, method features, or contextual clues.
teacher_use: Students answer before the teacher reveal.

title: Question 3
back: Teacher cue: this is where assumptions, framing, models, or perspective usually appear.
teacher_use: Students answer before the teacher reveal.

title: Question 4
front: Circle one confidence level and justify it.
back: Teacher cue: confidence is data for the debrief, not a grade.
teacher_use: Students answer before the teacher reveal.

title: Question 5
front: Name one piece of missing information.
back: Teacher cue: push for method, source, context, comparison, or definitions.
teacher_use: Students answer before the teacher reveal.

title: Question 6
front: Answer using the activity as evidence.
back: Teacher cue: students should move from the classroom example to a general TOK issue.
teacher_use: Students answer before the teacher reveal.

title: Student Task
front: Use the stimulus cards to complete the observation/inference/evidence/confidence grid, then answer one TOK knowledge question connected to Mathematics.
back: How does notation shape mathematical knowledge?
teacher_use: Use as the main activity instruction.

title: Teacher Reveal
front: The key move is not the “right answer”; it is the moment students see how new evidence updates prior probability; it does not replace the background rate.
back: Strong response: separates observation from inference. | Strong response: names a method, source, model, language choice, context, or perspective. | Strong response: revises confidence when new evidence or a new criterion appears. | Weak response: gives only opinion or says “it depends” without criteria.
teacher_use: Use after students commit to an answer.
